description: PR6 commit 5 — CP-SAT stage telemetry. Mirrors pr5_cpsat_improve.json: two cross-course sections (CS101, CS102) sharing all 10 students across three slots and one lecture room. Test seeds both placements into the SAME slot (MON 08:00) via ORM, then calls optimise_current_timetable with run_local_search=False and run_chain_search=False so CP-SAT runs on the seeded state directly. The polisher's weighted-overlap proxy (shared*5) strongly prefers splitting the pair; the real evaluator confirms the improvement. With TIMETABLE_PR6_STAGE_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=True, the result must carry stage_telemetry.stage_ms.cpsat > 0 and stage_telemetry.stage_iterations.cpsat == 1; other stage keys stay zero at this commit.
notes: Guardrail: cpsat.iterations == 1 means solver.solve() was actually invoked. Paths that short-circuit earlier (no scenario, no sections to polish) leave both keys at 0.
Test seeds MON 08:00 for both sections (same slot → heavy overlap) so CP-SAT is guaranteed to find an improvement and run its solver.
Flag-off variant: TIMETABLE_PR6_STAGE_TELEMETRY_ENABLED=False must leave all five keys at 0 even though the polisher still runs and emits PR5 decision-trace entries.
